results.title: David Caiafa
highlights:
AI Center of Excellence Lead at [Con Edison](https://www.linkedin.com/company/con-edison)
...
I'm a cross-functional product strategist with a passion for driving adoption of emerging technologies—especially generative AI. With experience leading enterprise change management, technical enablement, and governance initiatives, I've helped operationalize tools like Microsoft Copilot and Power Platform across thousands of users. My approach blends structured product thinking with human-centered communications and data-driven reporting. Whether it's launching champions programs, building governance dashboards in Power BI, or co-developing platform transformation whitepapers, I bring the same goal: help teams unlock value through innovation. Currently pursuing my MBA at NYU Stern, I focus on Finance and Technology Operations to strengthen my product leadership toolkit. Let's connect if you're working on AI adoption, digital transformation, or building out your enterprise product roadmap.
...
#### AI Center of Excellence Lead (Current)
...
As the lead for the AI Center of Excellence, I have been tasked with consolidating and presenting the company's AI offerings into a site that is easily accessible and understandable for people ranging from never having used an AI tool, to building complex agents. Additionally, I am gamifying internal and external certifications and trainings to help employees serve as ambassadors to their organizations to help them leverage AI. This roll encompasses change management, communications, and product management for AI tools and will include proliferating Google's AI offerings across the company as well as positioning Microsoft and Google's AI offerings to paint a full picture for the company's Generative and Machine Learning AI capabilities.
...

ilot. I developed and executed a champions program to embed Copilot into daily workflows, drive awareness, and promote sustained usage across the organization. I
...

To support user enablement, I created a tiered training strategy, offering foundational tools as well as advanced departmental sessions focused on operationalizing workflows using Copilot. These sessions were supported by dashboard-driven goal setting and strategic planning. Additionally, I partnered with Microsoft and Accenture to deliver workshops, distribute enablement materials, and amplify training efforts across the enterprise.

Senior Data Platform Analyst and Technical Lead at Con Edison with 3+ years of experience in enterprise data engineering, ETL pipeline development, API integration, cloud-native platform delivery, and cross-functional technical leadership. Currently serving as Technical Lead for a GCP-based enterprise AI construction analytics initiative within Con Edison's Enterprise Data Analytics Platform (EDAP) - overseeing data integration across Oracle EBS, Maximo, and DDH into cloud-based GCP pipelines, and acting as the technical bridge between internal teams and external vendor partners. Day to day, I work at the intersection of data engineering and business delivery - designing JavaScript and SQL-based ETL pipelines, resolving API integration issues across enterprise systems and serving as the primary technical point of contact for data and platform-related issues across multiple business units. Beyond my technical role, I am an MBA graduate of Baruch College's Zicklin School of Business, where I completed a consulting capstone advising a healthcare AI company on go-to-market strategy and product framework design, bridging my technical background with business strategy and executive communication.

...

...

...
HybridManagerML / AIData Engineering
...
Manager-level AI/ML engineering role at EY focused on designing and delivering scalable AI solutions for regulated power and utilities clients, with responsibility for leading technical teams and managing complex engagements.
...
Our Artificial Intelligence and Data team helps apply cutting-edge technology and techniques to bring solutions to our clients. As part of that, you'll sit side-by-side with clients and diverse teams from EY to create a well-rounded approach to advising and solving challenging problems, some of which have not been solved before. No two days will be the same, and with constant research and development, you'll find yourself building knowledge that can be applied across a wide range of projects now, and in the future. You'll need to have a passion for continuous learning, stay ahead of the trends, and influence new ways of working so you can position solutions in the most relevant and innovative way for our clients. You can expect heavy client interaction in a fast-paced environment and the opportunity to develop your own career path for your unique skills and ambitions. 
In this role, you will research, build, and implement scalable artificial intelligence systems that learn and make predictions tailored to business requirements across various environments, including cloud and on premises. You will enhance data pipelines and storage, ensuring data is clean, accurate, and optimized for XOps processes. Additionally, you will monitor and evaluate learning processes to continuously improve high-performance models, collaborating with other data and analytics professionals to industrialize analysis into effective analytics solutions.
...

You will work with a wide variety of clients to deliver the latest data science and big data technologies. Your teams will design and build scalable solutions that unify, enrich, and derive insights from varied data sources across a broad technology landscape. You will help our clients navigate the complex world of modern data science, analytics, and software engineering. We'll look to you to provide guidance and perform technical development tasks to ensure data science solutions are properly engineered and maintained to support the ongoing business needs of our clients.

We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.

Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities

, and fairness.

...

...

...

...

...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.

...

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
